findings.description: Downloading and running external code (curl | bash pattern).
findings.context:
5 | ## Install
   6 | 
   7 | ```bash
»  8 | curl -sL skil.sh | sh -s -- voxel51/fiftyone-skills
   9 | ```
  10 | 
  11 | When prompted, select **fiftyone-troubleshoot** from the menu.

findings.description: SKILL.md uses description-only framing patterns documented in Saha et al. (arXiv:2605.11418) §6 — claims of being 'best', 'always use', 'trusted by', 'source of truth' etc. — that bias an agent's selection in 77.6% of pairwise trials even when the underlying capability is unchanged. Strong manipulation surface for supply-chain attackers.
findings.context:
28 | 
  29 | Wait for explicit user confirmation before executing anything that modifies files, config, or data.
  30 | 
» 31 | ### 2. Never touch user datasets — FiftyOne is the source of truth
  32 | - Except when given explicit and direct instructions by the user:
  33 |     - NEVER modify, edit, delete, or clone user datasets as part of troubleshooting
  34 |     - NEVER call `fo.delete_dataset()`
findings.snippet: ### 2. Never touch user datasets — FiftyOne is the source of truth
